data.title: Tilray Earnings Call: Record Revenue, Real Risks Remain
data.description:
Tilray ((TLRY)) has held its Q2 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tilray’s Earnings Call Balances Record Revenue With Ongoing Risks

Tilray’s latest earnings call struck a cautiously optimistic tone, spotlighting record quarterly revenue, stronger margins in core cannabis and distribution businesses, and a substantially healthier cash position. Management repeatedly emphasized operational progress, significant cost savings, and improved cash flow, while openly acknowledging lingering challenges: continued GAAP net losses, a struggling beverage segment, regulatory uncertainty around U.S. hemp-derived THC, and pricing and permit pressures in parts of Europe. For investors, the call framed Tilray as a company making tangible financial strides but still executing a complex turnaround in a volatile regulatory and competitive landscape.

Record Quarterly Revenue Outperforms Expectations

Tilray reported record Q2 net revenue of $217.5 million (rounded to $218 million by management), comfortably ahead of analyst expectations despite headwinds in certain categories. The result underscores the breadth of Tilray’s multi-segment model—cannabis, beverages, distribution, and wellness—allowing strength in higher-growth areas like international cannabis and pharma distribution to offset weakness elsewhere. This top-line performance is notable given ongoing portfolio clean-up in beverages and regulatory friction in key markets, suggesting the core demand picture remains robust.

Profitability Metrics Improve, But Losses Persist

On profitability, Tilray delivered adjusted EBITDA of $8.4 million and positive adjusted cash operating income of $6 million for the quarter, but still posted an adjusted EPS loss of $0.02. The earnings call framed these figures as a step toward sustainable profitability, with management highlighting the move into positive cash operating territory as a key milestone. However, the modest year-over-year decline in adjusted EBITDA and the continued bottom-line loss underscore that Tilray has more work ahead before it can consistently generate profits under GAAP.

International Cannabis Drives Growth and Margins

Global cannabis net revenue reached $67.5 million, with international cannabis standing out as a major growth engine. International cannabis sales rose 36% year-over-year and 51% sequentially to roughly $20 million, largely driven by higher-margin markets outside Canada. Management is increasingly steering supply to these international channels, where regulatory frameworks support better pricing and more stable margin profiles. This strategic pivot is central to Tilray’s efforts to offset intense price compression in domestic flower markets and build a more profitable cannabis mix.

Tilray Pharma Distribution Delivers Record Quarter

Tilray’s distribution segment, branded Tilray Pharma, delivered its largest quarter ever with revenue of $85.3 million, up 26% year-over-year and 15% sequentially. The segment not only grew strongly but also improved gross margins, highlighting the attractiveness of medical and pharma-focused distribution versus more commoditized channels. Management positioned this business as a structural pillar of Tilray’s global cannabis and wellness strategy, helping stabilize revenue and provide consistent cash flow as the broader cannabis market evolves.

Balance Sheet Transitions to Net Cash Position

The company closed the quarter with $291.6 million in cash and marketable securities and trimmed debt by roughly $4 million in Q2, moving from a net debt stance to a net cash position of nearly $30 million. This shift is significant for equity investors in a capital-intensive, often cash-burning sector. Management underscored that a stronger balance sheet provides flexibility to navigate regulatory shifts, invest in higher-margin growth opportunities, and weather volatility without relying heavily on dilutive equity or expensive debt financing.

Cash Flow and Net Loss Show Material Improvement

Tilray’s cash flow used in operations improved sharply to $8.5 million from $40.7 million a year earlier, a $32.2 million swing that points to tighter working capital management and disciplined spending. Net loss narrowed to $43.5 million from $85.3 million, marking a 49% year-over-year improvement. While the company remains unprofitable, the trajectory shows that cost controls, mix shifts to higher-margin segments, and revenue growth are meaningfully shrinking the gap to break-even. Investors will likely watch whether these improvements can be sustained over the next several quarters.

Canadian Operations Scale and Cultivation Capacity Expand

In Canada, Tilray achieved its highest quarterly shipment volume in two years, surpassing 5.5 million units. The company also completed its Cayuga outdoor harvest, expanding cultivation capacity to about 200 metric tons annually, with broader Canadian capability referenced at up to roughly 270 metric tons. This expanded capacity positions Tilray to serve both domestic and international markets, particularly as it redeploys product toward higher-margin geographies. However, management also flagged that Canada remains a highly competitive market with ongoing price compression, which will require continued efficiency gains to protect margins.

Cost Savings Progress and Guidance Reaffirmation

Under its Project 420 cost-savings initiative, Tilray delivered $27 million of annualized cost reductions in the first half of the fiscal year, putting it on track to reach its $33 million target. Management emphasized operational discipline and reiterated its fiscal 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance of $62–$72 million, signaling confidence that efficiency measures and business mix improvements will translate into stronger profitability. These commitments suggest that cost control is not a one-off effort but a key component of the long-term strategy.

Tilray Medical’s Global Scale and R&D Focus

Tilray highlighted its medical cannabis franchise as a differentiator, with Tilray Medical expected to reach an annual run-rate of about $150 million. The business now offers more than 200 medical cannabis products, serves over 500,000 registered patients, and has participated in more than 25 clinical studies. Management presented this medical footprint as evidence of Tilray’s science-driven approach and as a platform for durable, higher-margin growth, particularly as more countries open up or expand their medical cannabis frameworks.

Margin Expansion Across Core Segments

Overall gross profit came in at $57.5 million, with a consolidated gross margin of 26%. Cannabis gross margin improved to 39%, up four percentage points from 35% a year ago, while distribution margins rose to 13% from 12% and wellness margins ticked up to 32% from 31%. These incremental gains reflect the company’s focus on higher-margin international cannabis markets, better pricing discipline, and portfolio rationalization. For investors, margin expansion in core segments is a key sign that Tilray’s strategy is gaining traction even as headline profitability remains constrained by certain underperforming businesses.

Beverage Segment Faces Headwinds and Integration Issues

Tilray’s beverage segment, which generated $50.1 million in net revenue, remained a sore spot on the call. The company is battling category-wide softness in craft beer, while ongoing portfolio optimization and integration efforts are weighing on performance and margins. Management acknowledged that margin construction in beverages is dragging down the overall gross margin and that “significant work” remains to complete brand turnarounds and fully capture synergies. The beverage business is still viewed as strategic for long-term diversification, but it is presently a drag on consolidated profitability.

Year-Over-Year EBITDA Softness Highlights Execution Challenge

Despite progress in many operating metrics, adjusted EBITDA slipped slightly to $8.4 million from $9.0 million a year earlier. The small decline signals that Tilray’s cost savings and margin improvements have not yet fully offset pressures from underperforming segments and ongoing investments in growth. The company portrayed this as a temporary setback amid structural improvements elsewhere, but from an investor’s perspective it reinforces the execution risk inherent in managing multiple business lines through a complex transformation.

Ongoing GAAP Net Loss Remains a Key Overhang

Tilray’s GAAP net loss of $43.5 million, while nearly halved from last year, remains a central concern for markets looking for a clear path to sustained profitability. Management framed the improved loss as evidence of underlying progress, but acknowledged that the company is not yet where it needs to be. Until Tilray can consistently produce positive net income, valuation will likely depend heavily on the market’s confidence in management’s ability to execute its cost savings, margin expansion, and growth initiatives.

Regulatory and Permit Challenges Pressure Growth

The company continues to grapple with regulatory delays and permit issues, particularly in Portugal and during transitions in Germany, along with broad price compression in flower. These headwinds create uncertainty in forecasting and complicate the rollout of new products and formats. Management reiterated that such hurdles are typical in emerging cannabis markets but acknowledged they can slow momentum and weigh on pricing. Investors should view these regulatory friction points as structural risks that can affect both volume and margin in key regions.

U.S. Hemp-Derived THC Regulation a Meaningful Risk

Tilray called out potential U.S. regulatory changes around intoxicating hemp products as a specific risk to its hemp-derived THC (HDD THC) business. The company is actively engaged in advocacy efforts, but the outcome remains uncertain and could result in lost revenue and job impacts if more restrictive rules are enacted. This introduces a binary element to part of Tilray’s U.S. strategy and underlines the broader theme that regulatory risk remains a defining feature of the cannabis and hemp sectors.

Portfolio Rationalization Weighs on Near-Term Revenue

Management noted that SKU rationalization and margin-focused initiatives, especially within beverages as part of Project 420, are intentionally depressing near-term revenue. Low-margin SKUs are being pulled or restructured, which delays top-line recovery but is expected to improve profitability over time. This trade-off highlights Tilray’s shift from pure growth to quality of growth, signaling to investors that management is prioritizing sustainable, higher-margin revenue over sheer volume.

Forward-Looking Guidance Signals Confidence in Margin-Led Growth

Looking ahead, Tilray reaffirmed its fiscal 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance of $62–$72 million and confirmed it is on track to reach $33 million in annualized cost savings from Project 420 by the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026, having already achieved $27 million in the first half. The company expects Tilray Medical to approach a roughly $150 million annual run rate and plans to triple Tilray Pharma’s pharmacy distribution footprint by adding about 3,000 pharmacies in fiscal 2026. Operationally, management will continue shifting supply to higher-margin international markets, scaling cultivation capacity around 200 metric tons annually, and leveraging a strengthened balance sheet—with $291.6 million in cash and marketable securities and a net cash position of about $30 million—to support both growth and resilience. These targets suggest a strategy centered on disciplined expansion, mix improvement, and tighter cost control rather than aggressive top-line pursuit at any price.

In closing, Tilray’s earnings call painted the picture of a company making steady, measurable progress, yet still working through a complex transformation. Record revenue, stronger margins in cannabis and distribution, and a move into a net cash position are clear positives for shareholders, while persistent GAAP losses, beverage underperformance, and regulatory uncertainty remain key watchpoints. For investors in cannabis and adjacent consumer categories, Tilray now looks more financially grounded, but its ultimate success will depend on executing its margin-focused strategy and navigating evolving regulations in North America and Europe.

data.title: Capstone Therapeutics Receives Nasdaq Minimum Bid Price Notice
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Capstone Therapeutics ( (CAPS) ) just unveiled an update.

On January 7, 2026, Capstone Holding Corp. received notice from Nasdaq that its common stock had closed below the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement for 30 consecutive business days, placing the company out of compliance with Nasdaq’s continued listing standards. The notification does not immediately affect trading of its shares, which continue to trade under the symbol “CAPS,” and Capstone has until July 6, 2026, to regain compliance, with the possibility of an additional 180-day extension if it meets other listing criteria; failure to do so could ultimately result in delisting, a development that would weigh on the stock’s liquidity and visibility for investors, while the company monitors its share price and considers options to restore compliance.

The most recent analyst rating on (CAPS) stock is a Hold with a $0.64 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Capstone Therapeutics stock, see the CAPS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CAPS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CAPS is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (declining revenue, shrinking margins, net losses) and high balance-sheet risk (negative equity), reinforced by bearish technicals (below major moving averages, negative MACD). Corporate events are a relative positive due to acquisition-driven growth initiatives and debt reduction, but they are not yet reflected in the underlying financial stability shown in the statements.

To see Spark’s full report on CAPS stock, click here.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

City Office REIT ( (CIO) ) has provided an update.

On January 9, 2026, a joint venture between Elliott Investment Management and Morning Calm Management, operating as MCME Carrell, completed its previously announced acquisition of City Office REIT for $7.00 per share in cash, leading to the delisting of the company’s common and preferred stock from the New York Stock Exchange and the planned suspension of its SEC reporting obligations. In connection with the closing, City Office REIT fully repaid and terminated its 2018 credit agreement, redeemed all outstanding 6.625% Series A preferred shares for $25.00 per share plus accrued distributions, cashed out and vested all outstanding equity awards, replaced its board and management with those of the acquisition vehicle, and converted the surviving entity’s governing documents, marking City Office’s transition from a publicly traded REIT to a privately held portfolio under MCME Carrell with the backing of Elliott and Morning Calm’s strategy to capitalize on opportunities in the U.S. commercial office market.

The most recent analyst rating on (CIO) stock is a Hold with a $7.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on City Office REIT stock, see the CIO Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Ducommun Settles Guaymas Fire Subrogation Claim for $4M
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from Ducommun ( (DCO) ) is now available.

On January 7, 2026, Ducommun Incorporated entered into a confidential settlement agreement to resolve a previously disclosed subrogation claim arising from a June 2020 fire at its performance center in Guaymas, Mexico, which had been pursued in an Arizona arbitration by the insurer of the entity providing labor and facilities for that site after payments to customer Williams International Co., LLC. Following a December 9, 2025 mediation, the parties agreed to dismiss the arbitration with prejudice and mutually release all past, present and future claims related to the fire in exchange for a $4 million payment by Ducommun, which the company plans to expense in the quarter ended December 31, 2025 and fund from existing cash, while denying any admission of liability and stating it believes no material claims remain outstanding from the incident aside from a potential Mexican insurer claim it considers time-barred.

The most recent analyst rating on (DCO) stock is a Buy with a $124.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ducommun stock, see the DCO Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DCO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DCO is a Neutral.

Ducommun’s overall stock score reflects a stable financial position with operational challenges, particularly in profitability. The technical indicators suggest bearish sentiment, and the valuation is unattractive due to negative earnings. Despite strong defense sector growth and improved liquidity, the litigation settlement and commercial aerospace challenges weigh on the score.

To see Spark’s full report on DCO stock, click here.

More about Ducommun

Ducommun Incorporated operates a performance center in Guaymas, Mexico that provides labor and facilities services, including work for customer Williams International Co., LLC, within the broader aerospace and defense manufacturing and services industry.

data.title: Genprex Regains Nasdaq Compliance but Faces Ongoing Monitoring
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Genprex ( (GNPX) ) has shared an update.

On January 7, 2026, Genprex, Inc. announced that it had regained compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum stockholders’ equity requirement of $2.5 million under Listing Rule 5550(b)(1), resolving a deficiency first flagged by Nasdaq in an August 19, 2025 notice. The company will remain under mandatory panel monitoring through January 7, 2027, and any renewed failure to meet the equity threshold during this period would trigger an automatic delisting determination without a further cure period, leaving Genprex to rely on a new hearing process and exposing shareholders to the heightened risk of the company’s securities being removed from the Nasdaq exchange.

data.title: Aprea Therapeutics Updates Oncology Pipeline and Strategy Outlook
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Aprea Therapeutics ( (APRE) ) has issued an update.

On January 9, 2026, Aprea Therapeutics updated its corporate presentation, outlining a development roadmap through 2026 that highlights multiple DDR-focused drug candidates and clinical milestones in early-stage oncology trials. The company detailed progress on its ATR inhibitor ATRN-119, for which further monotherapy enrollment has been paused in favor of exploring combination strategies, and on its WEE1 inhibitor APR-1051, which is being advanced toward clinical proof-of-concept as a potentially safer alternative in a target class historically limited by toxicity. The refreshed deck emphasizes Aprea’s strategy to capitalize on synthetic lethality as a validated cancer-killing approach, positioning the firm to benefit from rising global cancer incidence and sustained demand for targeted therapies, while showcasing an experienced management team and board with deep expertise in oncology drug development and commercialization.

The most recent analyst rating on (APRE) stock is a Hold with a $0.89 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Aprea Therapeutics stock, see the APRE Stock Forecast page.

The score is primarily constrained by weak financial performance (persistent losses and cash burn) and bearish technicals (price below key moving averages with negative MACD). Valuation is also unattractive due to losses and no dividend, while recent financing and early clinical progress provide only a modest offset.

To see Spark’s full report on APRE stock, click here.

More about Aprea Therapeutics

Aprea Therapeutics is a precision oncology company focused on synthetic lethality and DNA damage response (DDR) inhibition, developing highly targeted cancer therapies designed to destroy tumor cells by blocking their repair pathways while sparing healthy tissue. Its pipeline centers on next-generation DDR agents, including an ATR inhibitor (ATRN-119) and a WEE1 inhibitor (APR-1051), aimed at biomarker-selected patient populations within the large and growing oncology market.

data.title: Monte Rosa Therapeutics Prices $300 Million Public Offering
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Monte Rosa Therapeutics ( (GLUE) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On January 8, 2026, Monte Rosa Therapeutics priced a $300 million underwritten public offering comprising 11,125,000 shares of common stock at $24.00 per share and pre-funded warrants for 1,375,000 additional shares at $23.9999 per warrant, with underwriters granted a 30-day option to purchase up to 1,875,000 more shares. The deal, sold entirely by the company and expected to close on or about January 12, 2026, should generate approximately $281.6 million in net proceeds, which Monte Rosa plans to use, alongside existing cash and marketable securities, to advance its MRT-8102 program in chronic inflammatory diseases including atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease, further develop and deploy its QuEEN platform for additional molecular glue degraders in immunology, inflammation and oncology, and fund working capital and general corporate purposes; as a result of the capital raise, the company now expects its cash runway to extend into 2029, bolstering its financial position as it progresses three clinical programs and seeks to strengthen its leadership in the emerging MGD therapeutic space.

The most recent analyst rating on (GLUE) stock is a Buy with a $28.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Monte Rosa Therapeutics stock, see the GLUE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GLUE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GLUE is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by the sharp improvement in TTM fundamentals—especially very strong free cash flow and a swing to profitability—supported by positive pipeline and partnership news. These strengths are tempered by overbought technicals and a high P/E that increases sensitivity to any slowdown or reversal in the recent financial inflection.

To see Spark’s full report on GLUE stock, click here.

More about Monte Rosa Therapeutics

Monte Rosa Therapeutics is a Boston-based, clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing highly selective molecular glue degrader (MGD) medicines for patients with serious diseases. Using its proprietary QuEEN (Quantitative and Engineered Elimination of Neosubstrates) discovery engine, which integrates AI-guided chemistry, structural biology and proteomics, the company is building an industry-leading pipeline of first-in-class and only-in-class MGDs across autoimmune and inflammatory diseases, oncology and other indications, supported by collaborations with major pharmaceutical partners in immunology, oncology and neurology.

data.title: Lumen Technologies Upsizes Senior Notes to Restructure Debt
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Lumen Technologies ( (LUMN) ) has issued an announcement.

On January 9, 2026, Level 3 Financing, a wholly owned subsidiary of Lumen Technologies, completed an upsized $650 million issuance of additional 8.500% Senior Notes due 2036, bringing the total size of this series to $1.9 billion, with proceeds primarily used to fund the purchase of existing second-lien notes tendered in recently concluded cash tender offers and to cover related interest, fees and expenses, with any remaining funds earmarked for general corporate purposes. The new senior unsecured notes, which carry guarantees from the parent and key domestic subsidiaries and include customary covenants, redemption options and change-of-control protections, are part of a broader balance-sheet restructuring that also saw, as of December 23, 2025 and January 8, 2026, the execution of supplemental indentures and consent-driven amendments that strip most restrictive covenants and collateral from several series of existing second-lien notes, signaling Lumen’s move to simplify its debt stack, increase financial flexibility and shift value from secured to unsecured creditors within its capital structure.

The most recent analyst rating on (LUMN) stock is a Sell with a $7.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Lumen Technologies stock, see the LUMN Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on LUMN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LUMN is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak fundamentals and balance sheet risk (declining revenue/profitability, high leverage and negative equity). This is partly offset by a more positive earnings outlook driven by cost savings, refinancing/interest reductions, and expanding PCF/NaaS deal momentum. Technicals are mixed with recent weakness but some oversold signals, while valuation is constrained by ongoing losses (negative P/E) and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on LUMN stock, click here.

More about Lumen Technologies

Lumen Technologies, through its subsidiary Level 3 Financing, operates in the telecommunications and network infrastructure industry, providing enterprise connectivity and related services via a portfolio of long-term debt instruments issued in U.S. capital markets. The company frequently uses secured and unsecured note offerings and liability management transactions, such as tender offers and covenant amendments, to refinance existing debt and adjust its capital structure.

data.title: Eureka Acquisition Extends SPAC Deadline with Sponsor Financing
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Eureka Acquisition Corp Class A ( (EURK) ) just unveiled an update.

On January 2, 2026, Eureka Acquisition Corp deposited a $150,000 monthly extension fee into its trust account, funded by its sponsor Hercules Capital Management Corp, allowing the SPAC to extend its deadline to complete an initial business combination from January 3, 2026 to February 3, 2026. In connection with this payment, the company issued a $150,000 unsecured, interest-free promissory note to the sponsor on January 5, 2026, which is repayable upon the completion of a business combination or the company’s expiry and may be converted by the sponsor into private units at $10 per unit; a similar unsecured, interest-free promissory note for up to $300,000 was issued to the sponsor on January 6, 2026 to provide working capital until a deal is completed, also convertible into units. These financing steps extend Eureka’s runway to secure a target and provide operating liquidity, while potentially increasing the sponsor’s equity stake through conversion of the notes, with any resulting units subject to transfer restrictions until after the initial business combination.

More about Eureka Acquisition Corp Class A

Heron Therapeutics ( (HRTX) ) has shared an announcement.

On January 9, 2026, Heron Therapeutics reported preliminary, unaudited net revenue of approximately $40.5 million for the fourth quarter ended December 31, 2025, and about $154.9 million for full-year 2025, underscoring continued momentum across its acute care and oncology supportive care portfolio. Within Q4, CINVANTI remained the largest revenue contributor at roughly $22.9 million, while ZYNRELEF generated about $12.5 million and delivered the strongest quarter-over-quarter growth in the portfolio, rising around 35% versus the third quarter, with APONVIE and SUSTOL contributing approximately $3.8 million and $1.3 million, respectively; the solid top-line performance and particularly strong uptake of ZYNRELEF highlight progress in Heron’s acute care franchise and suggest strengthening commercial execution heading into the company’s final audited results.

data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

An announcement from Venu Holding Corporation ( (VENU) ) is now available.

On January 5, 2026, Venu Holding Corporation amended its existing letter of intent with Aramark Sports and Entertainment Services, expanding Aramark’s role as exclusive provider of food, beverage, catering, concession, retail, custodial, grounds, and facility maintenance services to cover two additional amphitheaters the company plans to build in El Paso and the greater Houston area, on the same terms as its Ford Amphitheater in Colorado Springs and Sunset Amphitheaters in McKinney and Tulsa, and extending Aramark’s right of first refusal on services at future venues as long as it retains its preferred or converted common stock holdings. In tandem with this operational expansion, Aramark committed an additional $10.005 million equity investment via 667 new shares of Series B 4% Cumulative Convertible Preferred Stock to be issued in two tranches in 2026, prompting Venu to file on January 6, 2026 an amendment to increase the authorized Series B Preferred Stock from 675 to 1,342 shares without changing its rights or preferences, and to confirm that Aramark’s registration rights for underlying common stock also apply to these new shares, strengthening Venu’s capital base and deepening its strategic alliance with a major venue services provider.
